tweets.text: thank god i grinded to colemak 150wpm with the zsa moonlander when i was a teen despite most people arguing wpm and twms were useless for coding back then.. Because now we’re coding in natural intuitive language and keyboard navigation directly impacts learning speed and output
much moreso than when the biggest bottleneck was spending 2 hours on stackoverflow understanding infra and logic
warp is so useful. having my work scattered across terminal windows used to create so much paralysing ADHD uncertainty, fear of losing context, too much task switching. now we are CRUISING
oh man i'm having so much fun building
how do i connect with the really cool people at the frontiers of technologies now
